subject: SIP weekly update - week ending 3 Apr
deck_filename: SIP weekly update - week ending 3 Apr.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian and all,

Pls find attached: 1) SIP weekly update; 2) Swarm weekly update.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:

spending more?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

SIP


Slide 3

- for standalone p&l, we would need to adjust if it is indeed to PH
spending more? Wei Loong Yong <weiloong.yong@shopee.com>


Per management guidance, SIP will spend more in PH for Mar-Jun as a
co-investment trial.

   -

   Mar target was set before the decision was finalized, resulting in a one-off
   mismatch and higher-than-expected Mar’26 target.
   -

   Apr-Jun targets have been updated to reflect the increased PH investment
   in the latest budget submission.


Table 1: SIP standalone P&L - PH import view (lower in Mar-Jun trial period)

[image: image.png]


- on ADO front, should we be overchieving if PH is spending more? Ailin Xie
<ailin.xie@shopee.com>


PH topline hit 100% RR in Mar, despite ~$70k additional spend.


The expected ADO uplift from this spend has not materialized yet, for 2
reasons:

Spend only started in the 2nd half of Mar to incubate new SKUs; new SKUs
   typically take 1-2 months to ramp up.

Ads CPO higher than BAU months as more spending shifted to non-ID lanes
   during Raya holiday, which have higher ads CPO vs ID lanes.


In Apr, as seasonal effects normalize and new SKUs mature, we expect +1.3k
ADO from $120k incremental spend (incremental CPO = $3). Overall topline
CB% +0.3pp vs Jan.


slide 9

- not much movement for #2 and #3? Ren Jiahui <jiahui.ren@shopee.com>


Both were slow moving due to Voucher PM team was focusing on higher impact
P0 Key Projects till end April while both of our requests are P1 Key
Projects based on RX team assessment.


On #2 Direct seller voucher sync, PM team has not enough resources to
review BRD, delayed to mid April, we are pushing PM to start PRD right
after BRD confirmation. On #3 Local SIP seller voucher sync, we are asking
if PM can start PRD in early May after #2 PRD is done. Will follow up with
PM again by early next week.


slide 11

- for the lanes that have gone live, what is the ADO % of SKUs that have
gone live? Are we leaving anything on the table? Wei Qiang
<wei.qiang@shopee.com>


For BN lanes: 100% of SIP-MY SKU and ADO covered - All SIP-MY shops and
SKUs have been whitelisted to be available to BN buyers.


For VNKH/THLA lanes: ADO% of live SKUs is 67% for VNKH and 52% for THLA.
This is driven by 1) an “express opt-in” approach similar to MYBN, and 2)
fewer prohibited categories set by local PMO. Remaining gaps in VNKH/THLA
are largely structural (e.g., category limits, sellers submitted opt-out
form), with limited incremental onboarding upside.


slide 13

- has this been resolved? Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>


In progress. Legal is drafting updated TOS to obtain seller consent for
data transfer, ETA 9 Apr. Launch (start selling) on 22 Apr remains on track.


slide 14

- success rate seems low? Wei Qiang <wei.qiang@shopee.com>


THVN’s acceptance rate of 38% is above expectation. Local SIP typically
sees only 20-30% acceptance, due to it

content.question:
slide 19

- for each market, what is direct % of total export ADO?

In general, our
direct does not feel like it is growing much right now Joanne Lim Xinyi
<joanne.limxy@shopee.com> How Sai Kit <saikit.how@shopee.com>



MTD regionally Direct is* 20% of total export ADO (vs Jan export% +7pp, CB
penetration +1pp to 1.7%)*, and *28% total export ADG (vs Jan export%
+10pp, CB penetration +1.5pp to 2.8%)*. Growth in ADO share in all export
markets vs Jan; led by MY, ID, and TH.

   - MY Direct: 44% (+12pp vs Jan, +6.6k ADO)
   - ID Direct: 13% (+5pp, +3.7k)
   - TH Direct: 11% (+8pp, +1.3k)
   - VN Direct: 16% (+2pp, +0.4k)
   - PH Direct: 2% (+2pp, +80)
   - SG Direct: 46% (+19pp, +60)



I think we can do better for ID?
Probably need to be at VN scale?

Overall on track against the *Dec’26 targets of 23% export ADO and 33%
export ADG (65k ADO and $1M ADG)*.



Table 2: Direct selling share of export ADO/ADG by export market





others

- for overall SIP, how can we structurally go to sellers to get them to
reduce price or give us more revenue so we can reduce price for them?

i am
not sure we are going about this enough to make our overall skus the most
competitive, especially if we are doing the heavy lifting on behalf on the
sellers Ailin Xie <ailin.xie@shopee.com>



Structurally there are 3 levers to obtain seller investment

   - *Lever 1 - system features to replicate local shop investment (impact
   potential high, scalability high);* including,


   - *Seller discounted price*: 1) BAU discount - already live; 2) spike
      day discount (ETA Q3)
      - *Seller voucher*: highlighted above (ETA end Jun)
      - *Seller paid ads*: for sellers enrolled in local mandatory ads
      program, charge the same % escrow for their SIP orders (ETA end Jun)


   - *Lever 2 -  system features to co-share SIP operational cost with
   seller (impact potential high, scalability to be assessed)*


   - Return chats to seller (ETA Q3), planned pilot in AME lanes
      - Return RR cost to seller (ETA Q3), planned pilot in AME lanes


   - *Lever 3 - manual seller negotiation (impact potential low,
   scalability low)*


   - Trialled with ~5.7k top export sellers (50% of SIP ADO) - only 6%
      agreed to a 5–20% price reduction, yielding +826 normalised ADO.
      - Impact was limited by export team capacity constraints and low
      seller ownership of overseas growth.



For SIP sellers, what fees do they pay now?

Do they pay import or export
country fees?
Should we charge them?
And use it to reduce item price/vouchers etc?

Operationally they are not doing anything and still getting orders right
now.



As of now, we are *doubling down on Lever 1* for scalable impact, with
plans to *monitor the Lever 2 pilot* in AME markets.



Swarm



slide 2 -3

- do we have apr targets?

Jialin Tan <jialin.tan@shopee.com>



For Apr, we are on track to 1.8k ADO (+33% MoM), $24k ADG (+24% MoM);
seller count +93% MoM, SKU count +76% MoM.



UE targets -$1.03, lower than Mar as we have more new SKUs to incubate in
Apr.





slide 4

- what does leads mean?

it is more important to understand how many sellers
we are onboarding?
And how it compares vs March?

Jialin Tan
<jialin.tan@shopee.com>



*“Leads”* refers to sellers who expressed interest by submitting the Swarm
Onboarding Survey. There are 3 steps in Swarm seller funnel: Leads
(indicated interest) >> Onboarded sellers (opened shop) >> Active sellers
(selling with active listings). We are optimizing each step to improve
conversion to *active sellers*, which is the core acquisition KPI.



Active sellers increased from 372 (Mar) to 462 (Apr MTD),* +90 MoM*; *on
track to hit +265 new active sellers Apr target.* Growth is mainly driven
by more ERP partners and mass pitching.



Table 4: Swarm ERP seller acquisition performance Apr MTD across channels



*Active seller #*

Mar Actual

Apr MTD

Apr Target

MoM (Target)

MoM (MTD)

Total

372

462

637

+265

+90

... ERP Extensions

72

115

228

+156

+43

... Mass Pitching

132

173

238

+106

+41

... Marketing Pitching

157

161

157

-

+4

... SAI Pitching

11

13

14

+3

+2



others

- I remember we were going to optimize our spend on unique assortment vs
assortment in general. How is that coming along?

Yuanfeng Wu
<yuanfeng.wu@shopee.com>



Yes we have *shifted marketing spend and acquisition focus *to unique and
price competitive SKUs *since mid-Mar*.



Actualized P&L data for orders generated from mid-Mar started to come in
this week (~3 weeks lag time). We are in progress of analyzing data, to be
shared via email by EOW.



- for overall CN SIP in general, how is our ability to negotiate with
sellers and reduce selling cost overall?

Linda Sun <linda.sun@shopee.com>



Overall, *CNSIP’s ability to negotiate and reduce selling cost is weak*,
with a low pitching success rate of ~1.3% even during campaign periods.



This is mainly driven by structural constraints:

   - *Fragmented long-tail supply* - most sellers and SKUs are long-tail
   with low topline, limits collective bargaining power
   - *Derived settlement pricing* - settlement prices are estimated based
   on sellers’ other markets and may not align with seller expectations,
   creating understanding gap before negotiation can even begin
   - *Limited pitching resources* - heavy reliance on mass pitching, with
   minimal 1:1 engagement



As a result, *seller-level negotiation is not scalable*. Cost reduction is
better achieved through a *program-level pricing design (e.g., admin fees*).
However we believe the current priority is still on maximizing seller/SKU
supply, over revenue from sellers.



Does it make sense for SIP sellers who has been onboard for a while?

I
think we should start charging for our services especially for sellers who
have used it for a while. And use it to increase conversion/earn more P&L



In contrast,* Swarm achieves higher negotiation success due to its flexible
pricing logic*:

   - *Settlement price control *- Sellers can see and adjust settlement
   prices directly, allowing for easier price concessions.



How come this cannot be done for CB non-swarm sellers?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,
Please find our responses in green.

slide 19

- for each market, what is direct % of total export ADO? In general, our
direct does not feel like it is growing much right now



MTD regionally Direct is 20% of total export ADO (vs Jan export% +7pp, CB
penetration +1pp to 1.7%), and 28% total export ADG (vs Jan export% +10pp,
CB penetration +1.5pp to 2.8%). Growth in ADO share in all export markets
vs Jan; led by MY, ID, and TH.

   -

   MY Direct: 44% (+12pp vs Jan, +6.6k ADO)
   -

   ID Direct: 13% (+5pp, +3.7k)
   -

   TH Direct: 11% (+8pp, +1.3k)
   -

   VN Direct: 16% (+2pp, +0.4k)
   -

   PH Direct: 2% (+2pp, +80)
   -

   SG Direct: 46% (+19pp, +60)



I think we can do better for ID? Probably need to be at VN scale?


Yes, ID is under-penetrated, driven by Raya seasonality. In Feb-Mar, we
intentionally slowed down seller acquisition and investment push, given
seller and RM capacity constraints during the Raya period (local raya push
focus + holiday absence).

From Apr, we are re-accelerating mass seller acquisition, RM pitching, and
Direct ads growth program (5% escrow in exchange of 5% PRM). Target to
bring ID direct share to ~16% by May.


others

- for overall SIP, how can we structurally go to sellers to get them to
reduce price or give us more revenue so we can reduce price for them? i am
not sure we are going about this enough to make our overall skus the most
competitive, especially if we are doing the heavy lifting on behalf on the
sellers



Structurally there are 3 levers to obtain seller investment

   -

   Lever 1 - system features to replicate local shop investment (impact
   potential high, scalability high); including,


   -

   Seller discounted price: 1) BAU discount - already live; 2) spike day
   discount (ETA Q3)
   -

   Seller voucher: highlighted above (ETA end Jun)
   -

   Seller paid ads: for sellers enrolled in local mandatory ads program,
